key_insight: HEDGEHOG shows that most molecular generators produce many candidates that fail realistic, multi-stage drug-likeness and feasibility checks, not just single-metric screens.
clinical_relevance: This benchmark can better triage early AI-generated compounds before expensive experimental follow-up, helping prioritize more plausible cancer drug candidates.
method: Six-stage filtration pipeline: preprocessing, physicochemical screening, structural alerts/sanity checks, synthesis feasibility, docking, and 3D pose/interactions.
Standardized evaluation of 23 molecular generators across three model classes.
Designed to mirror industrial hit-identification workflows rather than isolated benchmark metrics.
evidence_signal: Across 230,000 generated molecules, only 0.65% passed all six stages, indicating a steep drop-off under rigorous evaluation.
watch_item: Results depend on the chosen filters and docking/pose models, so the benchmark may still miss some valid chemotypes or over-reject novel scaffolds.
paper_title: HEDGEHOG: Hierarchical Evaluation of Drug Generators Through Rigorous Filtration
